---
title: "WDI 2022: Quick Findings"
author: "Hank Standaert"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Title slide

A quick summary of 2022 World Development Indicators (WDI).  
Source: World Bank WDI [@worldbank_wdi].

---

## What’s in the data?

- 217 countries, year = 2022
- Indicators include GDP per capita, inflation, growth, life expectancy, etc.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("wdi.csv")
df.shape
```

# GDP per capita distribution

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure()
df["gdp_per_capita"].dropna().plot(kind="hist", bins=30)
plt.title("GDP per capital distribution (2022)")
plt.xlabel("GDP per capital (current US$)")
plt.ylabel("Number of countries")
plt.show()
```

# Inflation vs GDP growth

```{python}
sub = df[["inflation_rate","gdp_growth_rate"]].dropna()
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(sub["inflation_rate"], sub["gdp_growth_rate"])
plt.title("Inflation vs GDP growth (2022)")
plt.xlabel("Inflation rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("GDP growth rate (%)")
plt.show()
```

# Key summary table

```{python}
stats = df[["gdp_per_capita","inflation_rate","gdp_growth_rate","life_expectancy"]].describe().T
stats = stats[["count","mean","std","min","50%","max"]]
stats.round(2)
```
